article_title: Extension to direct delivery from off-site storage

briefs.title: BC Liquor Manufacturers Can Deliver Directly to Customers Until June 30, 2027
briefs.summary: BC's Liquor and Cannabis Regulation Branch has extended the rule allowing liquor and cannabis manufacturers to deliver products directly to retail customers from their on-site store or off-site storage facilities. This temporary permission — which was set to expire June 30, 2025 — now runs until June 30, 2027. Deliveries can be made using your own staff or a third-party delivery service, but customers must still place orders through your store, online, or by phone. No physical sales are allowed at off-site storage locations.
briefs.action_required: Update your delivery procedures to reflect the new June 30, 2027 expiry date under Bulletin 25-03.

key_dates.label: BC LCRB direct delivery authorization expiry — Manufacturer licence extension

briefs.article_title: Extension to direct delivery from off-site storage
